messages.content: IC Markets’ competitive pricing and scalable execution make it an excellent option for algorithmic traders. IC Markets delivers an impressive range of social copy-trading platforms and platform plugins, and the broker’s research and educational offerings have recently improved to become more competitive with its industry peers.-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

IC Markets' algorithmic traders greatly benefit from its competitive pricing and scalable execution. The broker offers a wide range of social copy-trading platforms and platform plugins, making it an excellent choice. Additionally, IC Markets has recently enhanced its research and educational resources, positioning itself more competitively among industry peers.

Maintains regulatory status in one tier-1 jurisdiction and one tier-2 jurisdiction, making it a safe broker (average-risk) for trading forex and CFDs.
Offers competitive pricing across all account types with low average spreads.
Third-party research and trading tool plugins enhance the IC Markets MetaTrader offering.
IC Markets was our top-ranked MetaTrader broker for our 2023 Annual Awards.
The available range of markets has significantly expanded from 204 symbols to more than 3,500.
IC Markets’ low-cost, scalable execution makes it a perfect fit for traders who want to run algorithmic strategies.
Research and educational content has been improved with the addition of new video series and blog articles.
The newly added IC Social mobile app (powered by Pelican Exchange) complements IC Markets' variety of social copy trading apps.-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

IC Markets is a safe broker for trading forex and CFDs, as it holds regulatory status in both a tier-1 and tier-2 jurisdiction, ensuring an average-risk profile. The broker offers competitive pricing with low average spreads across all account types. The MetaTrader offering from IC Markets is further enhanced with third-party research and trading tool plugins. In fact, IC Markets was recognized as the top-ranked MetaTrader broker in our 2023 Annual Awards. Notably, the range of markets available has significantly increased, expanding from 204 symbols to over 3,500. For traders who utilize algorithmic strategies, IC Markets' low-cost and scalable execution provides an excellent fit. The research and educational content have also been improved with the addition of new video series and blog articles. Additionally, the newly introduced IC Social mobile app, powered by Pelican Exchange, adds to the variety of social copy trading apps offered by IC Markets.

Offering of investments
IC Markets offers a total of 3,583 tradeable instruments across its Australian, Cyprus, and Seychelles-based entities. The table below summarizes the different investment products available to IC Markets clients.

Cryptocurrency: Cryptocurrency trading is available at IC Markets through CFDs, but not available through trading the underlying asset (e.g. buying Bitcoin). Note: Crypto CFDs are not available to retail traders from any broker's U.K. entity, nor to U.K. residents.-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

IC Markets provides a wide range of investment opportunities, offering a total of 3,583 tradeable instruments through its entities based in Australia, Cyprus, and Seychelles. Clients can engage in cryptocurrency trading through CFDs; however, trading the actual underlying asset, such as purchasing Bitcoin, is not available. It's important to note that retail traders from any broker's U.K. entity, as well as U.K. residents, cannot access crypto CFDs.

Commissions and fees
Considering the small effective spreads and the low deposit requirement for its commission-based accounts, IC Markets’ trading costs rival the best low-cost forex brokers, such as IG and CMC Markets.

Global entity: Spreads at IC Markets vary depending on the trading platform you use, your account type, and your chosen IC Markets entity. IC Markets has three regulatory entities available: European (based in Cyprus), Australian, or its offshore entity based in Seychelles.

Account types: IC Markets offers three account options: the commission-free Standard account (spread only), and the commission-based Raw Spread and cTrader Raw Spread accounts.

cTrader versus MetaTrader accounts: The cTrader Raw Spread and the IC Markets (MT4 or MT5) Raw Spread accounts have a near-identical commission structure. The difference in pricing between the two comes down to platform choice. The cTrader-linked account commission is lower by about a tenth of a pip, at $3.0 per side for every 100,000 units, or $6.00 per RT. The Raw Spread account, which uses MT4 or MT5, charges $3.5 per side for every 100,000 units (1 standard lot) or $7.00 per round turn (RT).

Effective spreads: IC Markets lists its average spread on the EUR/USD at 0.02 pips (August 2021). After adding the commission equivalent of 0.6 pips for the cTrader account, the all-in cost comes to 0.62 pips (or 0.72 on MetaTrader in the Raw Spread account) – which is competitive. Alternatively, the Standard account lists an average spread of 0.62 pips and is commission-free, making it an attractive option.

Active traders: IC Markets offers discounts to traders that trade over 100 standard lots per month.

Agency and dealer execution: IC Markets provides execution on an agency basis, which means orders may be subject to positive or negative slippage – there are no requotes and no instant execution. That said, IC Markets is still considered a dealer and counterparty to your trade. Though it routes orders to nearly two dozen liquidity providers, it does not hedge all trades and thus does manage some risk as a counterparty (dealing desk).-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

When it comes to trading costs, IC Markets rivals top low-cost forex brokers like IG and CMC Markets, thanks to its small effective spreads and low deposit requirement for commission-based accounts.

Spreads at IC Markets vary depending on factors such as the trading platform, account type, and chosen regulatory entity (European, Australian, or offshore entity in Seychelles). IC Markets offers three account options: the commission-free Standard account with spreads only, and the commission-based Raw Spread and cTrader Raw Spread accounts.

For cTrader and MetaTrader accounts, the commission structure is nearly identical, with a slightly lower commission for the cTrader-linked account ($3.0 per side for 100,000 units) compared to the Raw Spread account ($3.5 per side for 100,000 units).

IC Markets states an average spread of 0.02 pips on the EUR/USD (August 2021), with an additional commission equivalent of 0.6 pips for the cTrader account. This brings the all-in cost to 0.62 pips (or 0.72 pips on MetaTrader in the Raw Spread account), which is competitive. Alternatively, the commission-free Standard account offers an average spread of 0.62 pips.

Active traders who trade over 100 standard lots per month can benefit from discounts offered by IC Markets.

IC Markets executes trades on an agency basis, which means orders can experience positive or negative slippage. The broker acts as a dealer and counterparty to trades, though it routes orders to multiple liquidity providers. While IC Markets manages some risk as a counterparty, it is not a dealing desk and does not provide requotes or instant execution.

Mobile trading apps
IC Markets provides mobile apps for the three platforms it offers – MetaTrader 4 (MT4), MetaTrader 5 (MT5), and cTrader – all of which come standard for iOS and Android. The lineup is good; however, it should be noted that the leaders in mobile (e.g., Saxo Bank, CMC Markets, TD Ameritrade) all offer proprietary trading apps.

Tools comparison: The MetaTrader and cTrader suite have similar mobile functionality and charting. The platform you choose will likely depend on whether you trade manually or use desktop platforms for automated trading systems.

Algorithmic trading: Automated trading is not possible with the mobile versions of the cTrader and MetaTrader platforms. That means they are primarily useful for managing existing positions or manually opening new trades. Copy trading is available on mobile with supported third-party platforms such as ZuluTrade and Myfxbook.-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

IC Markets offers mobile trading apps for all three of its platforms - MetaTrader 4 (MT4), MetaTrader 5 (MT5), and cTrader. These apps are available for both iOS and Android devices. While IC Markets' mobile app lineup is good, it's worth noting that other industry leaders such as Saxo Bank, CMC Markets, and TD Ameritrade offer proprietary trading apps.

When comparing the tools, both the MetaTrader and cTrader mobile apps provide similar functionality and charting capabilities. The choice of platform may depend on whether you prefer manual trading or use desktop platforms for automated trading systems.

It is important to note that automated trading is not possible on the mobile versions of the cTrader and MetaTrader platforms. These mobile apps are primarily designed for managing existing positions or manually opening new trades. However, copy trading is available on mobile through supported third-party platforms like ZuluTrade and Myfxbook.

Other trading platforms
IC Markets has strengthened its position as an excellent choice for algorithmic traders thanks to the availability of both the MetaTrader and cTrader platforms, as well as its execution methods (i.e., no requotes) and the ability to place orders within the spread.

IC Markets was our pick for the best MetaTrader broker for our 2023 Annual Awards. Want to learn more about MetaTrader? Read our full MetaTrader guide, and check out our MetaTrader 5 (MT5) guide if you want to dive deep into the newest version of the MetaTrader platform.

Platforms overview: IC Markets offers the MetaTrader 4 (MT4) and MetaTrader 5 (MT5) trading platforms developed by MetaQuotes Software Corp., alongside the cTrader platform developed by Spotware Systems. Additional add-ons available for MetaTrader clients include the Advanced Trading Tools package for the desktop version, along with plugins from AutoChartist and Trading Central. While these third-party plugins must be installed separately, their ability to enhance the MetaTrader experience makes them worth the extra effort.

Charting: Charts on MetaTrader and cTrader are excellent, with a good variety of default indicators pre-installed and an even larger array available from third-party providers. The IC Markets cTrader platform offering includes both the web-based and desktop versions.

MetaTrader Tools: IC Markets provides access to the Advanced Trading Tools package, which is a suite of platform add-ons developed by FX Blue LLP. IC Markets also provides installable third-party plugins from Trading Central and AutoChartist.

Algorithmic trading: The desktop version of MetaTrader supports algorithmic trading, and the cTrader suite includes trading applications such as cAlgo for automated trading, and cTrader Copy for social copy trading. IC Markets is popular with serious algorithmic traders due to its low cost and execution methods, and its support for Virtual Private Servers (VPS).

Interested in learning about algorithmic trading and high-frequency trading systems? Check out our high-frequency trading guide.

VPS services: For traders that complete at least 15 standard lots, IC Markets offers several free third-party Virtual Private Servers (VPS) services for hosting client algorithms.

Social copy trading: In addition to cTrader Copy and the native Signals market from MetaTrader, IC Markets offers the Myfxbook (AutoTrade) and ZuluTrade platforms for copy trading across its global brands. IC Markets recently launched IC Social, a mobile-only app powered by Pelican Exchange. This additional social-copy trading platform is available in select regions from IC Markets' Seychelles entity.-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

IC Markets has established itself as an excellent choice for algorithmic traders by offering both the MetaTrader and cTrader platforms, along with features like no requotes and the ability to place orders within the spread.

In fact, IC Markets was recognized as the best MetaTrader broker in our 2023 Annual Awards. If you want to learn more about MetaTrader, we have a comprehensive MetaTrader guide available, as well as a dedicated guide for MetaTrader 5 (MT5) for a deeper dive into the newest version of the platform.

IC Markets provides access to the MetaTrader 4 (MT4) and MetaTrader 5 (MT5) platforms developed by MetaQuotes Software Corp, in addition to the cTrader platform created by Spotware Systems. MetaTrader clients can enhance their trading experience with additional add-ons such as the Advanced Trading Tools package for the desktop version, along with plugins from AutoChartist and Trading Central. While these plugins require separate installation, their ability to enhance the MetaTrader experience justifies the extra effort.

Both MetaTrader and cTrader platforms offer excellent charting features, with a wide range of default indicators and even more options available from third-party providers. IC Markets provides access to both the web-based and desktop versions of the cTrader platform.

Algorithmic trading is well-supported on the desktop version of MetaTrader, and the cTrader suite includes trading applications like cAlgo for automated trading and cTrader Copy for social copy trading. IC Markets is especially popular among serious algorithmic traders due to its low cost, execution methods, and support for Virtual Private Servers (VPS).

For traders who complete at least 15 standard lots, IC Markets offers several free third-party Virtual Private Servers (VPS) services for hosting client algorithms.

IC Markets caters to social copy trading as well, with offerings like cTrader Copy and the native Signals market on MetaTrader. Additionally, IC Markets provides access to the Myfxbook (AutoTrade) and ZuluTrade platforms for copy trading across its brands worldwide. Recently, IC Markets introduced IC Social, a mobile-only app powered by Pelican Exchange, as an additional social copy trading platform available in select regions through its Seychelles entity.

Market research
Though IC Markets has shown improvement with daily articles and its Web TV section, research remains a weak point in its overall offering compared to the best brokers in this category, such as IG and Saxo Bank.

Research overview: IC Markets posts several articles each day on its blog, including a technical and fundamental forecast. I found the quality of the research content to be pretty standard, though not quite as rich as what you see from industry leaders such as IG and Saxo Bank.

Market news and analysis: IC Markets delivers in-house technical and fundamental analysis content by way of its blog, and provides daily updates in its market analysis section. There are videos from Trading Central and short clips that recap market events under the Web TV section, as well as live trading sessions posted to the IC Markets YouTube channel.-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

Whilst IC Markets has made improvements in its research offering, particularly with its daily articles and Web TV section, it still falls short compared to top brokers in the market research category like IG and Saxo Bank.

In terms of research, IC Markets publishes several articles on a daily basis on its blog, including technical and fundamental forecasts. However, the quality of their research content is considered average and not as comprehensive as what is offered by industry leaders like IG and Saxo Bank.

For market news and analysis, IC Markets provides in-house technical and fundamental analysis content through its blog. They also offer daily updates in their market analysis section. Additionally, there are videos provided by Trading Central and short clips that summarize market events under the Web TV section. Furthermore, IC Markets has live trading sessions available on their YouTube channel.

Overall, while IC Markets has made efforts to enhance its research offerings, it still has room for improvement compared to the best brokers in terms of market research.

Education
IC Markets has a good range of content for beginner as well as advanced traders, with over 100 written articles and dozens of videos. However, content is not organized by experience level, and there is no progress tracking or quizzes, which are both features I’ve come to expect from the best forex brokers in this category.

Learning center: The educational content at IC Markets is organized into six series, such as Forex Trading 101 and Trading Plan 101. The quality of the articles are a touch above the industry average. IC Markets has also created a podcast series known as “IC Your Trade”, and has a YouTube channel that hosts a catalog of archived webinars.

Room for improvement: IC Markets could enhance its educational offering by developing courses that feature progress tracking and quizzes, and by adding the ability to filter content by experience level.-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

IC Markets offers a decent selection of educational content for both beginner and advanced traders, including over 100 written articles and numerous videos. However, the content is not categorized according to experience level, and lacks features like progress tracking and quizzes that are commonly found in the educational offerings of top forex brokers.

The learning center at IC Markets organizes educational content into six series, such as Forex Trading 101 and Trading Plan 101. The quality of the articles is slightly above average compared to the industry standard. IC Markets also provides a podcast series called "IC Your Trade" and maintains a YouTube channel that hosts a collection of archived webinars.

There is room for improvement in IC Markets' educational offering. To enhance the learning experience, the broker could develop structured courses that include features like progress tracking and quizzes. Additionally, adding the ability to filter content based on a trader's experience level would be beneficial.

IC Markets appeals primarily to algorithmic traders that use the MT4, MT5, or cTrader platforms, and who need agency execution coupled with competitive spreads and low commissions.

Catering to high-frequency traders helps IC Markets achieve significantly higher trading volumes relative to its peers, as automated trading strategies can typically do higher turnover than manual traders.

Access to third-party social trading services like Myfxbook and ZuluTrade helps to round out IC Markets’ overall offering and make it appealing to retail forex and CFD traders, but I generally do not recommend IC Markets unless you are an algorithmic trader or if your manual trading strategy is spread-sensitive.IC Markets is a good broker for investors who want to run automated trading strategies on MetaTrader and cTrader platforms. IC Markets provides social copy trading platforms and a growing selection of research and educational materials, and offers competitive pricing.IC Markets is regulated in Australia, Cyprus, the Bahamas, and Seychelles; the degree of safety of your funds will depend on which entity holds your funds.

For example, in Australia, the National Guarantee Fund does not provide any protection when trading CFDs, but IC Markets maintains indemnity insurance and holds your funds in a Client Moneys Trust Account). For clients of IC Markets in Cyprus, the Investors Compensation Fund can protect eligible retail clients in the unlikely event of bankruptcy, for up to 20,000 euros per account held by IC Markets EU.

IC Markets was founded in 2007, and factors such as its years of operation and scope of regulatory licenses should be taken into consideration when choosing a broker. Going with a broker that is well-capitalized also reduces your potential counterparty risk.-Help me rework this paragraph to keep the same meaning

IC Markets primarily caters to algorithmic traders who utilize the MT4, MT5, or cTrader platforms and require agency execution, competitive spreads, and low commissions. This focus on high-frequency traders allows IC Markets to achieve higher trading volumes compared to its competitors, as automated trading strategies can generate higher turnover.

The inclusion of third-party social trading services like Myfxbook and ZuluTrade complements
